name: spawn
description: Spawn another beige agent (or spawn a sub-agent of yourself) and hold a multi-turn conversation with it. Each call returns the target agent's full response plus a session key you can pass back to --session to continue the same conversation. Depth is enforced per-target. All spawns are opt-in — no targets are allowed until the targets config is set.
commands: --target <agent>  --session <key> --message-file <path> — Continue with a longer message from a file
--info                                      — Show which agents you may spawn and at what depth

targets.description: Map of target agent names to their config. Each key is an agent name that may be spawned. The special key "SELF" resolves to the calling agent's own name at runtime, enabling sub-agent patterns without hardcoding names. Per-agent restrictions are handled via beige's toolConfigs override mechanism. Absent = no spawns permitted.

maxDepth.description: Default maximum nesting depth for targets that don't specify their own. 0 = no spawns allowed. 1 (default) = agents may spawn agents, but those sub-agents may not spawn further agents. Individual targets can override this.
